acceptanceCriteria: scripts/package-plugin.mjs adds collectSeedFiles(seedRoot): a strict recursive walk of the entire seed that rejects any extra file, extra directory, symlink, non-regular node, or unexpected executable bit (only bin/rigor may be executable) and requires all 21 allowlisted files; collectPluginFiles (producer) is unchanged.

scripts/install-verified.mjs extracts the verified in-memory archiveBytes via a private temp file (not the on-disk archive path) and validates the seed with collectSeedFiles before promotion; an injected root file/dir/symlink now fails closed with a refuse receipt and no seed left on disk.

test/provenance.test.ts adds collectSeedFiles negative cases (extra root file, extra directory, symlink, unexpected exec bit, missing file) and the exact-tree accept case; npm run test:all (374 tests) is rerun and reported in the PR; recorded git-diff-check passes.

.github/workflows/consumer-portability.yml adds a negative integration step: each consumer Node builds an archive with an injected root .mcp.json and asserts the wrapper refuses (non-zero) and leaves no seed; workflow still requests only contents: read.
docs/provenance.md, docs/threat-model.md, docs/adr/0001-provenance-trust-model.md describe the exact-tree strict check and verified-bytes extraction.
constraints: Do not change the attestation trust decision or the producer collectPluginFiles behavior; the strict check is consumer-only.
Preserve #26 cross-Node portability (tar-layer digest comparison) and all existing negative cases.
The CI workflow must request no write permissions and must not create tags, releases, or attestations.
No src/**, dist/**, schema, or policy changes; scripts/tests/docs/workflow only.
R5 applies independent-review remediations within the R4 allowedPaths only; no new files or scope.
stopConditions: an irreversible or external write is needed
credentials or personal data are encountered
